init.title: The largest federation in Brazil: now modern
init.subtitle: CAFe 2.0 has arrived to revolutionize digital access between institutes and universities.
about.title: CAFe connects a network of more than 300 federated institutions.
about.p1: Maintained by RNP, the current CAFe operates the technological infrastructure that connects hundreds of Brazilian universities, institutes, and research centers.
about.p2.prefix: The proposal of CAFe 2.0 aims to modernize the current model through the
about.p2.suffix: protocol, expanding the reach of the federated network and increasing the quality of benefits for all parties.
faq.title: FAQ
faq.a1: CAFe 2.0 is currently in the pilot implementation phase. You can follow progress in the Roadmap section or subscribe to our list to receive updates.
faq.q2: Who is the target audience for CAFe 2.0?
faq.a2: CAFe 2.0 solutions serve students, professors, and researchers through centralized service discovery. The Benefits Portal is publicly accessible, with no institutional affiliation required to explore the available services. In addition, the Management System is designed for interaction with institution managers and federation operators.
faq.q3: What changes for those who already use CAFe today?
faq.a3: CAFe 2.0 is being developed to coexist with the current federation during the transition. For end users, the most visible change will be a simpler and more intuitive WAYF. For institutional IT managers and application maintainers, the new Management System simplifies the registration and maintenance of identity providers.
faq.a4: It is a digital service discovery platform available to the Brazilian academic community, accessible through institutional login.
faq.q5: How can I contact the project?
faq.a5: You can use the subscription form below to send a message to the team. Dedicated support channels will be made available as the project advances toward production.
faq.q6: Which international initiatives are aligned with CAFe 2.0?
faq.a6: CAFe 2.0 adopts OpenID Federation 1.0, a standard being studied by eduGAIN and implemented by national federations such as those in Italy and Sweden. This positions the Brazilian federation in line with the global movement to modernize academic identity management.
features.card1.title: Three platforms, one solution
features.card1.text: The adoption of the OpenID Federation protocol enables a flexible infrastructure with the creation of authentication, administrative management, and user-facing portals.
features.card2.title:
Connection with 
Brazil and the World
features.card2.text: The solution provides interoperability with eduGAIN, connecting the Brazilian federation to services from more than 3400 international institutions.
